what_it_does: A workflow script (build-only, no commit) that dispatches two parallel agent lanes to build the repo-exocortex composition: LANE INGEST builds a new exocortex_ingest.py that deterministically walks ~/company into per-file units and runs a bounded capture+embed into corpus space='repo' (reusing existing engine seams), and LANE GROUND builds organ-map-grounded.md by verifying each row of the FRAME organ-map against the real code (by query or grep) and computing a real built-percentage. It defines binding LAWS, an output JSON SCHEMA, runs the two lanes via parallel(), and logs the result.
observations: Header comments label it COMPOSITION ①, BUILD-ONLY, with the next loop beat committing
Declares export const meta with name, description, and a single 'Build' phase
LAWS string mandates reading AGENTS.md -> MAP.md -> STATE.md -> module AGENTS.md + two cognition-self-improvement docs first

LAW-0 UNIFICATION enumerates specific reuse seams with file:line: Suite.capture_corpus (suite.py:9630), cognition.embed_corpus_to_spaces (:376), store/vector_index.build_index (:64), Suite.find_relations (:9734), Suite.list_corpus (:9717), roles/repo_digest.py — with REUSE-DON'T-PARALLEL
Embedder discipline: load via run_role(ensure=True)/capture embed-on-write; co-resides with chat-4b (~13GB on 16GB card, check `company gpu`, surface swap-approval [G14], never silent-evict
Floor discipline: ingest/query is run:// COMPUTATION emitting no resolve/approve/dispatch, launching no claude -p; lineage gate required before first capture (fail-loud)
Anti-hardcoding rules: chunk-size from model context not a constant; file-set from a deterministic walk; NO green-paint (a real embed that didn't persist -> say so)
File-disjoint enforcement: each lane works only its lane's files, do NOT edit runtime/cognition.py|suite.py|roles.py|mcp_face/* (read-only for the reuse seam), do NOT commit
SCHEMA requires lane/files_written/how_verified/status with status enum built-verified|built-untested|blocked, plus findings/needs_tim/notes

LANE INGEST: new exocortex_ingest.py walks named dirs into units {path, kind, text, sha, module_home}, skips .git/binaries/node_modules/.venv, then captures a ~25-40 file subset via Suite.capture_corpus(project=company, session=exocortex-1, round=1, projection=repo) reusing repo_digest, verifying by list_corpus + a real-neighbour query

LANE GROUND: new organ-map-grounded.md verifies each FRAME row as ✅ EXISTS / 🟡 PARTIAL / 🔴 NET-NEW with file:symbol cites, computes the real built-percentage, flags overclaimed/wrong FRAME rows, degrades to grep if the corpus isn't queryable yet
standouts: LAW-0 REUSE-DON'T-PARALLEL is enforced concretely by listing the exact existing engine seams (file:line) the agent must reuse rather than re-build a second corpus/embed/index path
The two lanes are designed file-disjoint and complementary: INGEST builds the embedded corpus, GROUND uses it (or grep) to verify-and-correct the FRAME organ-map and produce the real NET-NEW backlog
Explicit NO-green-paint and fail-loud rules: a real embed that didn't persist must be reported, lineage gate required before first capture, never silent-evict the co-resident model
conventions: Follows the workflow-script convention (meta + phases, LAWS preamble, JSON output SCHEMA, parallel() lanes, log())
Follows the floor / run://-computation / propose-only discipline
Follows registry-is-truth / no-hardcoding (chunk-size and file-set derived, not constant)
Embeds binding-law reading order and file-disjoint lane boundaries as agent instructions
concerns: Hardcodes specific file:line reuse-seam locations (suite.py:9630 etc.) in the LAWS string — these drift if the source files change
GPU figures (~13GB, 16GB card, 7GB free) are stated as point-in-time facts in the instructions and could go stale
notes: This is the build-only workflow that stands up the repo-exocortex: one lane ingests+embeds the repo into a queryable corpus, the other grounds the FRAME organ-map against real code — both reusing existing engine seams, file-disjoint, propose-only, no commit.
questions: Did both lanes complete, and did the FRAME organ-map's '~80% built' claim hold up against the grounded cites?
What was the full file-count the deterministic walk found (the known-next full-ingest size)?
Did the bounded embed persist real vectors and return real neighbours (the keystone verification)?
purpose_vs_actual: Matches — the header states COMPOSITION ① build-only repo-exocortex (ingest+embed corpus + ground the organ-map, no commit), and the two-lane parallel body implements exactly that with the stated reuse, floor, and file-disjoint constraints.
apparent_themes: Reuse-don't-parallel / LAW-0 unification (no second corpus/embed/index path)
Grounding claims against real code (verified cites over guessed percentages)
intention_signals: Trying to turn the repo into a queryable embedded exocortex by reusing the existing capture/embed/index engine, and to use that (or grep) to convert an unverified lean organ-map into a grounded one with real file:symbol cites and a real NET-NEW build backlog.
novelty: The keystone idea of ingesting the repo + Surface docs into an embedded space='repo' corpus and then grounding a self-describing organ-map by querying that corpus, with the workflow enforcing reuse of named existing seams rather than building a parallel path.
contribution: Contributes the orchestration that builds the repo-exocortex (deterministic walk + bounded embed-capture reusing existing engine seams) and the grounded organ-map verification lane, with concrete reuse-seam citations and floor/embedder governance encoded as agent laws.
summary_for_embedding: A build-only two-lane workflow script that builds exocortex_ingest.py to walk and embed the repo into corpus space='repo' (reusing capture_corpus/embed/index seams) and organ-map-grounded.md to verify the FRAME organ-map against real code with file:symbol cites.
intention_for_embedding: Built to stand up a queryable embedded repo exocortex by reusing the existing engine and to ground an unverified organ-map into real file:symbol cites plus a real NET-NEW backlog, file-disjoint and without committing.
